mainEntity.name: How much does a P510 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Twin-Turbo) engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned P510 twin-turbo typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, varying by specification. Used units can be found from £2,800–£4,800, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the P510 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Twin-Turbo) a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P510 is a modern, high-performance engine (4.0★) with promising real-world feedback. However, its high state of tune and twin-turbo configuration place additional stress on the cooling system. Regular maintenance is absolutely essential. Long-term reliability data is still emerging.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common P510 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system stress is the primary concern — the high-output tune generates significant heat, requiring meticulous coolant system maintenance. Charge air cooler leaks and turbo actuator issues have been reported on early examples.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the P510?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P510 powers the Range Rover (L460), Range Rover Sport (L461), and Defender (L663) — representing the highest-output Ingenium petrol variant at 510 PS.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover P510 the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the P510 is shared with Jaguar fitments (F-Pace SVR-spec variants). The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The P510 is JLR's most powerful straight‑six petrol — immense performance with twin‑turbo urgency — but cooling system stress is a concern — buy with full JLR service history.
* Best for: enthusiasts wanting the ultimate Ingenium petrol experience with 510 PS and V8‑rivalling performance.
rows.value: * Avoid if: you're looking at an early example with no documented cooling system history or one that has been tracked without proper maintenance.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a P510 twin‑turbo typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [INDUSTRY ESTIMATE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Land Rover and Jaguar applications; labour time varies by model and engine bay access.

items.question: How much does a P510 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Twin-Turbo) engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned P510 twin‑turbo typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, varying by specification. Used units can be found from £2,800–£4,800, but provenance is critical. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Is the P510 (Ingenium 3.0 I6 Twin-Turbo) a reliable engine?
items.answer: The P510 is a modern, high‑performance engine (4.0★) with promising real‑world feedback. However, its high state of tune and twin‑turbo configuration place additional stress on the cooling system. Regular maintenance is absolutely essential. Long‑term reliability data is still emerging.

items.question: What is the most common P510 failure?
items.answer: Cooling system stress is the primary concern — the high‑output tune generates significant heat, requiring meticulous coolant system maintenance. Charge air cooler leaks and turbo actuator issues have been reported on early examples. The MHEV system (48V) has also had some software‑related glitches.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the P510?
items.answer: The P510 powers the Range Rover (L460), Range Rover Sport (L461), and Defender (L663) — representing the highest‑output Ingenium petrol variant at 510 PS.

items.question: Is the Land Rover P510 the same as Jaguar's version?
items.answer: Yes — the P510 is shared with Jaguar fitments (F‑Pace SVR‑spec variants). The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.
